pdg_node.statement: (signal: 1-D numeric array or None, sampling_rate: int or float) -> tuple[1-D numeric array, float]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Validates that the signal is provided and casts sampling_rate to float, raising a TypeError if signal is None.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Designs a 1st-order Butterworth low-pass filter at 8 Hz cutoff using the given sampling rate, returning second-order sections (SOS) coefficients for zero-phase filtering.

declaration.name: design_lowpass_filter
declaration.docstring: Designs a 1st-order Butterworth low-pass filter at 8 Hz cutoff using the given sampling rate, returning second-order sections (SOS) coefficients for zero-phase filtering.

declaration.name: design_lowpass_filter
declaration.docstring: Designs a 1st-order Butterworth low-pass filter at 8 Hz cutoff using the given sampling rate, returning second-order sections (SOS) coefficients for zero-phase filtering.

declaration.name: design_lowpass_filter
declaration.docstring: Designs a 1st-order Butterworth low-pass filter at 8 Hz cutoff using the given sampling rate, returning second-order sections (SOS) coefficients for zero-phase filtering.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Transforms the time-domain signal into the log-magnitude domain by taking the element-wise absolute value followed by the natural logarithm, preparing it for linear (cepstral) filtering.

declaration.name: compute_log_magnitude
declaration.docstring: Transforms the time-domain signal into the log-magnitude domain by taking the element-wise absolute value followed by the natural logarithm, preparing it for linear (cepstral) filtering.

declaration.name: compute_log_magnitude
declaration.docstring: Transforms the time-domain signal into the log-magnitude domain by taking the element-wise absolute value followed by the natural logarithm, preparing it for linear (cepstral) filtering.

declaration.name: compute_log_magnitude
declaration.docstring: Transforms the time-domain signal into the log-magnitude domain by taking the element-wise absolute value followed by the natural logarithm, preparing it for linear (cepstral) filtering.

pdg_node.statement: (sos_coefficients: 2-D numeric array, log_magnitude_signal: 1-D float array) -> 1-D float array
pdg_node.informal_desc: Applies the designed low-pass SOS filter to the log-magnitude signal using zero-phase forward-backward filtering (sosfiltfilt), effectively retaining the slowly varying envelope component while rejecting high-frequency oscillations in the log domain.

declaration.name: apply_lowpass_filter_in_log_domain
declaration.type_signature: (sos_coefficients: 2-D numeric array, log_magnitude_signal: 1-D float array) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Applies the designed low-pass SOS filter to the log-magnitude signal using zero-phase forward-backward filtering (sosfiltfilt), effectively retaining the slowly varying envelope component while rejecting high-frequency oscillations in the log domain.

declaration.name: apply_lowpass_filter_in_log_domain
declaration.type_signature: (sos_coefficients: 2-D numeric array, log_magnitude_signal: 1-D float array) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Applies the designed low-pass SOS filter to the log-magnitude signal using zero-phase forward-backward filtering (sosfiltfilt), effectively retaining the slowly varying envelope component while rejecting high-frequency oscillations in the log domain.

declaration.name: apply_lowpass_filter_in_log_domain
declaration.type_signature: (sos_coefficients: 2-D numeric array, log_magnitude_signal: 1-D float array) -> 1-D float array
declaration.docstring: Applies the designed low-pass SOS filter to the log-magnitude signal using zero-phase forward-backward filtering (sosfiltfilt), effectively retaining the slowly varying envelope component while rejecting high-frequency oscillations in the log domain.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Maps the filtered log-domain signal back to the amplitude domain by applying the element-wise exponential, recovering the homomorphic (non-normalized) envelope of the original signal.

declaration.name: exponentiate_to_envelope
declaration.docstring: Maps the filtered log-domain signal back to the amplitude domain by applying the element-wise exponential, recovering the homomorphic (non-normalized) envelope of the original signal.
